text: How many people visited the Great Exhibition of 1851?
zh: 1851年的大博览会共吸引了多少参观者？

text: Perhaps the most extraordinary building of the 19th century was the Crystal Palace
zh: 19世纪最非凡的建筑或许就是水晶宫，

text: which was built in Hyde Park for the Great Exhibition of 1851.
zh: 它是为1851年的大博览会而建，坐落于海德公园。

text: The Crystal Palace was different from all other buildings in the world, for it was made of iron and glass.
zh: 水晶宫与世界上其他任何建筑都不同，因为它是用钢铁和玻璃建造而成的。

text: It was one of the biggest buildings of all time and a lot of people from many countries came to see it.
zh: 它是当时最大的建筑之一，吸引了许多国家的人前来参观。

text: A great many goods were sent to the exhibition from various parts of the world.
zh: 来自世界各地的大量商品被送到博览会展出。

text: There was also a great deal of machinery on display.
zh: 展会上还展示了大量机械。

text: The most wonderful piece of machinery on show was Nasmyth's steam hammer.
zh: 展出的机械中最令人惊叹的是内史密斯的蒸汽锤。

text: Though in those days travelling was not as easy as it is today,
zh: 尽管在那个年代，出行远不如今天这般便利，

text: steam boats carried thousands of visitors across the Channel from Europe.
zh: 蒸汽船仍载着成千上万的游客从欧洲跨越海峡而来。

text: On arriving in England, they were, taken to the Crystal Palace by train.
zh: 他们抵达英国后，再乘火车前往水晶宫。

text: There were six million visitors in all,
zh: 参观者总数多达六百万人，

text: and the profits from the exhibition were used to build museums and colleges.
zh: 博览会所得的利润被用来兴建博物馆和学院。

text: Later, the Crystal Palace was moved to South London.
zh: 后来，水晶宫被迁往伦敦南部。

text: It remained one of the most famous buildings in the world until it was burnt down in 1936.
zh: 它一直是世界上最著名的建筑之一，直到1936年被大火烧毁。
